summary: P0 Bug Fix VERIFIED: Gray wrapper (rgb(242,242,242)) from SafeAreaProvider is now being properly overridden. The CSS injection fix in ThemeContext.tsx is working correctly. All 6 verification tests PASSED.

test_results.gray_wrapper_removed: PASS - No rgb(242,242,242) found in div hierarchy
test_results.theme_toggle_works: PASS - Dark ↔ Light switching works correctly

verification_details.fix_explanation: The CSS injection via style tag with !important rules successfully overrides react-native-web's default gray backgrounds on SafeAreaProvider wrapper divs.

fix_applied.description: Added CSS injection via dynamic style tag (id='theme-override-styles') that contains !important rules to override: html, body, #root backgrounds; SafeAreaProvider wrapper; any div with rgb(242,242,242); and react-native-web class .r-backgroundColor-14lw9ot
fix_applied.how_it_works: useEffect hook runs on effectiveTheme change, creates/updates style element with CSS rules using the current theme's background color

context_for_next_testing_agent: Dark mode fix is now working. The CSS injection in ThemeContext.tsx properly overrides react-native-web default backgrounds. No gray wrapper (rgb(242,242,242)) visible in dark mode. Theme switching between Dark and Light works correctly with proper body background colors.

comparison_with_previous_iteration.iteration_157_status: Style tag NOT injected, useEffect not executing, gray wrapper at div[5]
comparison_with_previous_iteration.iteration_158_status: Style tag INJECTED, useEffect executing properly, NO gray wrapper
comparison_with_previous_iteration.improvement: P0 BUG FIXED - Full dark mode now working
